name: Barbell Bench Press
overview: Press a barbell from chest level on a flat bench to build chest pressing strength and size.
coachingCues: Set your upper back and keep your chest lifted.
Lower the bar under control to the mid chest.
Keep wrists stacked over elbows through the press.
Drive the bar up in a slight arc without losing leg drive.
commonMistakes: Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Letting elbows flare excessively from the bottom.
Losing upper back tightness during the set.
Pressing with bent wrists and unstable forearms.

name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
overview: Press a barbell on an incline bench to emphasize the upper chest and front delts.
coachingCues: Set the bench to a moderate incline and lock your upper back in.
Lower the bar to the upper chest with elbows under the bar.
Keep the shoulder blades pulled down and back.
Press up smoothly without shrugging at the top.
commonMistakes: Using too steep an incline and turning it into a shoulder press.
Dropping the bar too high toward the neck.
Letting the shoulders roll forward at the bottom.
Bouncing out of the bottom position.

name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
overview: Press a barbell on a decline bench to bias the lower chest through a stable pressing path.
coachingCues: Secure your lower body and keep your torso locked to the bench.
Lower the bar to the lower chest with controlled speed.
Keep forearms vertical near the bottom.
Press back up without letting the shoulders drift forward.
commonMistakes: Setting up loosely on the decline bench.
Lowering the bar too high on the torso.
Flaring the elbows aggressively during the press.
Rushing the descent and losing control.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
overview: Press dumbbells from a flat bench to train the chest with a greater independent range of motion per arm.
coachingCues: Start with the dumbbells stacked over the elbows.
Lower with control until the upper arms reach a strong stretch.
Keep your shoulder blades pinned to the bench.
Press up evenly on both sides without clanging the bells together.
commonMistakes: Dropping too deep and losing shoulder position.
Letting one arm press faster than the other.
Shrugging the shoulders at lockout.
Using momentum to kick the weights through sticking points.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
overview: Press dumbbells on an incline bench to target the upper chest with added shoulder stability demand.
coachingCues: Set the bench to a moderate incline before lifting the dumbbells into position.
Keep elbows slightly tucked as you lower toward the upper chest.
Maintain a fixed ribcage and stable shoulder blades.
Press the dumbbells up on a smooth converging path.
commonMistakes: Turning the movement into a high-angle shoulder press.
Letting the dumbbells drift too far behind the shoulders.
Losing control at the bottom stretch.
Pressing unevenly from side to side.

name: Decline Dumbbell Press
overview: Press dumbbells on a decline bench to emphasize the lower chest with independent arm control.
coachingCues: Secure your position on the bench before starting the set.
Lower the dumbbells to a comfortable lower chest line.
Keep the wrists stacked and the elbows controlled.
Press back up without letting the shoulders roll forward.
commonMistakes: Starting with a loose setup on the decline.
Dropping the elbows too wide at the bottom.
Bouncing out of the stretched position.
Touching the dumbbells together at the top and losing tension.

name: Machine Chest Press
overview: Press the machine handles forward from a seated position to train the chest on a fixed path.
coachingCues: Adjust the seat so the handles line up with mid chest.
Keep your upper back against the pad throughout the rep.
Press through the palms without locking the shoulders forward.
Control the return until the chest is stretched.
commonMistakes: Setting the seat too high or too low.
Letting the shoulders round off the pad.
Shortening the range to move more load.
Slamming the weight stack between reps.

name: Incline Machine Chest Press
overview: Press the handles on an incline chest press machine to target the upper chest on a guided path.
coachingCues: Adjust the seat so the handles start near upper chest level.
Keep your ribcage stacked and shoulder blades anchored.
Press up and in without shrugging.
Use a full controlled return to keep tension on the chest.
commonMistakes: Using a seat height that turns the motion too low or too high.
Letting the elbows drift too far behind the torso.
Bouncing off the bottom stop.
Relaxing the chest between repetitions.

name: Smith Machine Bench Press
overview: Press the bar on a Smith machine from a flat bench to train the chest with extra stability.
coachingCues: Place the bench so the bar tracks comfortably toward mid chest.
Keep your upper back tight against the bench.
Lower the bar under control with elbows stacked under the wrists.
Drive straight up while keeping the chest high.
commonMistakes: Positioning the bench so the bar path feels forced on the shoulders.
Letting the elbows flare too early.
Dropping the bar too quickly into the bottom.
Relaxing the upper back because the bar is guided.

name: Smith Machine Incline Bench Press
overview: Press the Smith machine bar from an incline bench to emphasize the upper chest with a fixed bar path.
coachingCues: Set the bench so the bar lowers toward the upper chest comfortably.
Keep the shoulder blades pinned to the bench pad.
Lower with control and maintain stacked wrists and elbows.
Press up without letting the shoulders shrug toward the ears.
commonMistakes: Setting the bench angle too steep for a chest-dominant press.
Using a bench position that forces the bar path into the neck.
Unlocking the shoulders at the bottom.
Cutting the range of motion short to add load.

name: Weighted Dip
overview: Perform dips with added external load to build lower chest and triceps pressing strength.
coachingCues: Lean slightly forward and keep the chest open.
Lower until the shoulders tolerate a deep stretch without collapsing.
Keep the elbows tracking back instead of flaring wide.
Drive back up under control without swinging.
commonMistakes: Dropping too fast into the bottom position.
Shrugging the shoulders and losing scapular control.
Using momentum to start each rep.
Staying too upright if the goal is chest bias.

name: Assisted Dip
overview: Perform dips on an assisted machine to train the chest and triceps with reduced bodyweight load.
coachingCues: Select enough assistance to keep the reps controlled.
Lean slightly forward to keep tension on the chest.
Lower smoothly without dropping into the bottom.
Press back up while keeping the shoulders down.
commonMistakes: Using too much assistance and shortening the range.
Bouncing on the assistance platform.
Locking into an overly upright torso if chest bias is intended.
Letting the shoulders roll forward at the bottom.

name: Cable Chest Fly
overview: Sweep the cable handles inward in an arc to isolate the chest through a long range of tension.
coachingCues: Keep a soft bend in the elbows and hold that angle.
Bring the hands together by squeezing the chest, not by pressing.
Keep the ribcage stable and shoulders down.
Control the stretch on the way back.
commonMistakes: Turning the fly into a pressing motion.
Bending and straightening the elbows during the rep.
Letting the shoulders shrug forward.
Using momentum to swing the handles.

name: Low to High Cable Fly
overview: Sweep the cable handles upward and inward to bias the upper chest with continuous tension.
coachingCues: Start with the handles low and slightly behind the torso.
Raise the hands in an upward arc toward upper chest height.
Keep the elbows softly bent and fixed.
Pause briefly in the contracted position without shrugging.
commonMistakes: Pressing the handles instead of flying them together.
Using too much torso swing.
Lifting the shoulders toward the ears.
Taking the hands too high and losing chest tension.

name: High to Low Cable Fly
overview: Sweep the cable handles downward and inward to emphasize the lower chest through constant tension.
coachingCues: Start with the pulleys set high and the chest lifted.
Bring the hands down and together in a controlled arc.
Keep a slight elbow bend without pressing the load.
Let the chest stretch on the return without losing posture.
commonMistakes: Using a pressing pattern instead of a fly.
Rounding the shoulders as the hands come together.
Cutting the range short to chase heavier load.
Letting the elbows bend more as fatigue builds.

name: Single Arm Cable Chest Press
overview: Press one cable handle forward at a time to train the chest while resisting torso rotation.
coachingCues: Set your stance and brace before each rep.
Press straight forward without letting the torso twist.
Keep the shoulder blade stable as the arm extends.
Control the return until the chest is loaded again.
commonMistakes: Rotating the torso to move the handle.
Letting the shoulder roll forward at lockout.
Standing too narrow and losing balance.
Rushing the eccentric phase.

name: Push Up
overview: Press your body away from the floor to train the chest, triceps, and anterior shoulder with bodyweight resistance.
coachingCues: Keep a straight line from head to heel.
Lower the chest under control between the hands.
Let the shoulder blades move naturally without collapsing.
Press away from the floor while staying braced.
commonMistakes: Sagging the hips or lifting them too high.
Cutting the range of motion short.
Flaring the elbows excessively.
Dropping the head and losing spinal alignment.

name: Weighted Push Up
overview: Perform push ups with added external load to increase chest and triceps resistance.
coachingCues: Secure the load so your body stays stable through the set.
Brace hard and keep the torso rigid.
Lower until the chest is just above the floor.
Press up smoothly without shifting the hips.
commonMistakes: Letting the added load slide and break position.
Losing a straight body line as fatigue rises.
Using partial reps to handle more load.
Bouncing off the bottom.

name: Machine Chest Fly
overview: Bring the machine arms together in a hugging arc to isolate the chest on a fixed path.
coachingCues: Set the seat so the handles align with mid chest.
Keep the chest tall and shoulders down on the pad.
Move through a smooth arc and squeeze at the front.
Control the return into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders during the squeeze.
Slamming the machine arms together.
Using momentum to start the rep.
Setting the seat too high or too low.

name: Pull Up
overview: Pull your body upward to a bar with a pronated grip to build lat-focused vertical pulling strength.
coachingCues: Start from a dead hang with the ribs stacked and shoulders set.
Drive the elbows down toward your sides.
Keep the torso controlled instead of kipping.
Lower to a full hang under control.
commonMistakes: Swinging the body to gain momentum.
Pulling with the chin only and shortening the range.
Shrugging excessively through the rep.
Stopping short of a full controlled eccentric.

name: Weighted Pull Up
overview: Perform pull ups with added external load to build high-force lat and upper back strength.
coachingCues: Secure the added load before leaving the floor.
Start from a full hang and drive the elbows down hard.
Keep the torso controlled without kipping.
Lower with a full controlled eccentric each rep.
commonMistakes: Swinging the body to move the extra load.
Using partial reps instead of a full range.
Losing shoulder position at the bottom.
Letting the weight pull the lower body out of control.

name: Assisted Pull Up
overview: Use an assisted pull up machine to train the pull up pattern with reduced bodyweight resistance.
coachingCues: Choose enough assistance to keep the rep smooth and full range.
Start from a long hang with the chest tall.
Pull the elbows down toward the ribs.
Control the lowering phase instead of dropping back to the platform.
commonMistakes: Using too much assistance and turning the set into easy partials.
Bouncing off the knee platform.
Shrugging up instead of pulling through the back.
Stopping the eccentric early.

name: Chin Up
overview: Pull your body up with a supinated grip to train the lats with greater biceps involvement.
coachingCues: Begin from a full hang with palms facing you.
Drive the elbows down and slightly forward.
Keep the torso controlled and avoid kicking.
Lower to a full stretch under control.
commonMistakes: Using momentum to clear the bar.
Stopping short of the bottom hang.
Overextending the neck to finish the rep.
Letting the shoulders shrug up during the pull.

name: Weighted Chin Up
overview: Perform chin ups with added load to challenge the lats and elbow flexors with heavier resistance.
coachingCues: Secure the load before starting the set.
Use a dead hang start and pull with the elbows.
Keep the torso steady instead of swinging through the rep.
Lower all the way down with control.
commonMistakes: Using too much load and shortening the range.
Swinging the lower body to clear the bar.
Relaxing the shoulders abruptly at the bottom.
Leading only with the chin instead of pulling through the back.

name: Lat Pulldown
overview: Pull a cable bar from overhead to the upper chest to train the lats through a scalable vertical pull.
coachingCues: Set your thighs firmly under the pad before pulling.
Lead with the elbows and pull toward the upper chest.
Keep the torso only slightly leaned back.
Control the bar back to a full overhead stretch.
commonMistakes: Turning the movement into a row with too much torso lean.
Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Using momentum to start the rep.
Letting the shoulders dump forward at the top.

name: Machine Pulldown
overview: Pull the handles on a machine pulldown to train the lats on a fixed path with stable body support.
coachingCues: Set the seat and thigh support so you stay locked in.
Drive the elbows down toward the sides.
Keep the chest tall instead of curling into the pull.
Control the return to a long lat stretch.
commonMistakes: Jerking the first part of the rep.
Using short partials to move more load.
Shrugging the shoulders up through the pull.
Leaning excessively backward to turn it into a row.

name: Close Grip Lat Pulldown
overview: Pull a close neutral handle down toward the upper chest to train the lats through a compact pulling path.
coachingCues: Sit tall and lock the thighs under the pad.
Drive the elbows down and slightly in toward the torso.
Keep the chest lifted as the handle approaches the upper chest.
Reach back up fully without losing control.
commonMistakes: Leaning too far back and turning the rep into a row.
Stopping short of the stretch at the top.
Shrugging instead of depressing the shoulders.
Using momentum off the first rep.

name: Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
overview: Pull a wide overhand bar to the upper chest to emphasize the lats with reduced elbow flexor contribution.
coachingCues: Take a grip just outside shoulder width that still allows full range.
Pull the elbows down rather than yanking with the hands.
Keep the torso stable with only a small lean back.
Return the bar overhead under control.
commonMistakes: Using an excessively wide grip that shortens the range.
Pulling the bar behind the neck.
Swinging the torso to finish reps.
Letting the shoulders rise abruptly at the top.

name: Single Arm Lat Pulldown
overview: Pull one cable handle down at a time to train the lats unilaterally with a long overhead stretch.
coachingCues: Brace the torso before starting each rep.
Drive the elbow down toward the hip on the working side.
Avoid twisting the torso to finish the pull.
Reach fully overhead on the return.
commonMistakes: Rotating the body to move the handle.
Bending the elbow first instead of initiating with the back.
Cutting the top stretch short.
Shrugging the shoulder during the pull.

name: Barbell Bent Over Row
overview: Row a barbell from a bent-over position to build back thickness through a free-weight horizontal pull.
coachingCues: Hinge to a stable torso angle and brace the trunk hard.
Pull the bar toward the lower ribs or upper waist.
Lead with the elbows and squeeze the upper back at the top.
Lower the bar under control without changing torso position.
commonMistakes: Standing too upright and turning it into a shrug-row.
Using momentum from the hips to start each rep.
Rounding the lower back under load.
Letting the bar drift away from the body.

name: Pendlay Row
overview: Row a barbell explosively from the floor on each rep to build upper back power and thickness.
coachingCues: Set a flat torso with the bar starting from the floor each rep.
Pull the bar sharply toward the lower chest or upper abdomen.
Keep the trunk braced so the torso angle stays fixed.
Let the bar settle fully before the next rep.
commonMistakes: Using hip extension to heave the bar upward.
Letting the torso rise as the set gets hard.
Bouncing the plates instead of resetting.
Rounding the spine at the start.

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Row
overview: Row one dumbbell at a time from a bench-supported position to train the lats and mid back unilaterally.
coachingCues: Brace your non-working hand and knee firmly on the bench.
Pull the elbow back toward the hip or lower ribs.
Keep the torso stable without twisting to the top.
Lower the dumbbell into a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Rotating the torso to move the weight.
Shrugging the shoulder at the top.
Yanking the dumbbell with momentum.
Cutting the bottom stretch short.

name: Chest Supported Dumbbell Row
overview: Row dumbbells while supported on an incline bench to train the mid and upper back with less lower back demand.
coachingCues: Set the bench angle so the chest stays fully supported.
Pull the elbows up and back without lifting the chest off the pad.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower the dumbbells under control to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Lifting the chest off the bench to finish reps.
Shrugging instead of rowing through the back.
Using short partial reps.
Letting the dumbbells drift too far forward.

name: Seated Cable Row
overview: Row a cable attachment to the torso from a seated position to train the lats and mid back through a smooth resistance curve.
coachingCues: Sit tall with the chest up before initiating the pull.
Drive the elbows back without overextending the lower back.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together briefly at the finish.
Reach forward under control to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Rocking the torso to start each rep.
Pulling with the arms and losing back tension.
Shrugging the shoulders at the finish.
Cutting the stretch short on the way forward.

name: Single Arm Cable Row
overview: Row one cable handle at a time to train the lats and mid back while controlling torso rotation.
coachingCues: Brace the torso before pulling.
Drive the elbow back toward the hip without twisting.
Keep the shoulder down as the arm finishes the row.
Reach forward into a full stretch under control.
commonMistakes: Rotating the torso to move more load.
Shrugging the shoulder at the finish.
Jerking the first part of the rep.
Letting the cable yank the shoulder forward.

name: Machine Seated Row
overview: Row the machine handles toward the torso to train the back on a fixed seated path.
coachingCues: Set the seat and chest position so the handles start at full reach.
Pull the elbows back while keeping the chest tall.
Pause briefly in the contracted position.
Return under control without losing contact with the pad.
commonMistakes: Shrugging the shoulders during the pull.
Using short partial reps to move more weight.
Letting the chest collapse off the support.
Snapping the handles forward between reps.

name: Chest Supported Row Machine
overview: Row against a chest pad on a machine to emphasize the mid and upper back with minimal lower back fatigue.
coachingCues: Keep the chest firmly planted on the pad.
Drive the elbows back and slightly out to the line of pull.
Squeeze the upper back at the top without craning the neck.
Lower to a full controlled stretch.
commonMistakes: Lifting off the chest pad to finish reps.
Shrugging instead of retracting the shoulder blades.
Using too much load and shortening the range.
Jerking the handles from the bottom.

name: High Row Machine
overview: Row the machine handles from a high angle to train the lats and upper back through a converging path.
coachingCues: Set your chest against the pad before the first rep.
Drive the elbows down and back along the machine path.
Keep the shoulders packed instead of shrugging.
Control the return to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Pulling mostly with the arms and losing back tension.
Lifting the chest off the pad.
Using short partial reps to overload the machine.
Letting the shoulders roll forward at the bottom.

name: T Bar Row
overview: Row a T-bar style machine or lever to build lat and mid-back thickness with heavy loading potential.
coachingCues: Set your feet and torso position before initiating the pull.
Drive the elbows back along the line of the handle.
Keep the chest proud and the trunk braced.
Lower the load under control to a full stretch.
commonMistakes: Heaving the weight with excessive torso movement.
Shrugging instead of rowing through the back.
Letting the lower back round under fatigue.
Using too short a range to chase load.

name: Chest Supported T Bar Row
overview: Row a chest-supported T-bar setup to emphasize the mid and upper back while reducing spinal loading.
coachingCues: Keep the chest firmly against the support throughout the set.
Pull the elbows back without flaring them excessively.
Pause briefly at peak contraction.
Lower into a full stretch without bouncing off the bottom.
commonMistakes: Lifting off the pad to finish reps.
Using momentum instead of controlled rowing.
Shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Stopping short of the stretched position.

name: Dumbbell Pullover
overview: Lower a dumbbell overhead and pull it back over the torso to train the lats through shoulder extension.
coachingCues: Keep the ribcage stacked and elbows softly bent.
Lower the dumbbell overhead only as far as you can stay controlled.
Pull back over the torso by driving through the lats.
Keep the hips and lower back steady on the bench.
commonMistakes: Turning the movement into an elbow extension exercise.
Overarching the lower back to gain range.
Dropping too deep and losing shoulder control.
Rushing the stretched position.

name: Machine Pullover
overview: Pull the machine arms through a pullover path to isolate the lats with minimal elbow flexor involvement.
coachingCues: Set the machine so the shoulders start in a long overhead stretch.
Drive the arms down in an arc without bending the elbows much.
Keep the chest stable against the pad if present.
Control the return into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows too much and turning it into a row.
Using momentum off the start.
Shrugging the shoulders during the pull.
Cutting the stretch short.

name: Straight Arm Cable Pulldown
overview: Pull a cable bar down with nearly straight arms to isolate the lats through shoulder extension.
coachingCues: Hinge slightly and brace before initiating the pull.
Keep a small elbow bend and hold it throughout.
Drive the bar down by moving through the shoulders, not the elbows.
Return slowly to a full overhead stretch.
commonMistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a pulldown.
Using the torso to heave the load downward.
Shrugging into the top position.
Stopping the range short of a full stretch.

name: Inverted Row
overview: Pull your chest toward a fixed bar with your body suspended underneath to train the upper back and lats.
coachingCues: Set the bar height so you can maintain a rigid body line.
Pull the chest toward the bar by driving the elbows back.
Keep the hips up and the core braced.
Lower under control to a full arm extension.
commonMistakes: Letting the hips sag during the set.
Shrugging the shoulders instead of rowing.
Using short partial reps.
Leading with the chin instead of the chest.

name: Weighted Inverted Row
overview: Perform inverted rows with added external load to increase upper back and lat resistance.
coachingCues: Secure the load so your torso stays stable.
Pull the chest to the bar without letting the hips sag.
Keep the neck neutral and the body rigid.
Lower under control to full extension.
commonMistakes: Letting the added load shift and break body position.
Using hip thrust to finish the rep.
Shortening the range to handle more load.
Shrugging at the top.

name: Barbell Overhead Press
overview: Press a barbell overhead from shoulder level to build front delts and triceps strength.
coachingCues: Brace the trunk and squeeze the glutes before pressing.
Start with the bar over the upper chest and wrists stacked.
Press straight up while moving the head back and then through.
Lock out overhead without overextending the lower back.
commonMistakes: Leaning back excessively to finish the rep.
Letting the bar drift too far in front of the body.
Starting with loose wrists and elbows.
Using leg drive when the movement should stay strict.

name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
overview: Press dumbbells overhead from a seated position to train the front delts with extra torso stability.
coachingCues: Set the bench upright and keep your upper back supported.
Start with elbows under the wrists at shoulder height.
Press upward in a smooth path without banging the dumbbells together.
Lower under control to a strong bottom position.
commonMistakes: Arching away from the bench to finish reps.
Pressing unevenly from side to side.
Starting with the elbows too far behind the body.
Dropping too fast into the bottom.

name: Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press
overview: Press dumbbells overhead from a standing position to train the shoulders with added full-body stabilization demand.
coachingCues: Brace the trunk and keep the ribs down before each rep.
Press the dumbbells straight up over the shoulders.
Keep the wrists stacked and elbows under the bells.
Lower with control without leaning back.
commonMistakes: Overarching the lower back to finish reps.
Letting the dumbbells drift too far forward.
Pressing with loose wrists.
Using knee drive when the goal is a strict press.

name: Machine Shoulder Press
overview: Press the machine handles overhead to train the delts and triceps on a stable guided path.
coachingCues: Set the seat so the handles start around shoulder height.
Keep your back against the pad throughout the set.
Press up smoothly without shrugging the shoulders.
Lower under control to a consistent bottom position.
commonMistakes: Using a seat height that starts too low or too high.
Bouncing the weight stack between reps.
Letting the shoulders roll forward at the bottom.
Shortening the range to overload the machine.

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
overview: Press the Smith machine bar overhead to train the front delts and triceps with added stability.
coachingCues: Set the bench so the bar path clears your face comfortably.
Keep your upper back supported and wrists stacked.
Press straight up without shrugging into the ears.
Lower with control to a consistent shoulder-level bottom.
commonMistakes: Positioning the bench so the bar path forces the shoulders.
Overarching the lower back against the seat.
Using partial reps to overload the bar.
Letting the elbows drift too far behind the body.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
overview: Raise dumbbells out to the sides to isolate the side delts through shoulder abduction.
coachingCues: Keep a soft elbow bend throughout.
Lead the raise with the elbows, not the hands.
Stop where the side delts stay loaded without shrugging.
commonMistakes: Swinging the torso to start reps.
Shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
Turning the movement into a front raise.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
overview: Raise a cable handle out to the side to isolate the side delts with continuous tension.
coachingCues: Stand far enough from the stack to keep tension at the bottom.
Lead with the elbow as the arm rises.
Keep the torso quiet and the shoulder down.
commonMistakes: Twisting the torso to move the handle.
Shrugging through the top.
Using too much load and losing the side-delt path.

name: Single Arm Cable Lateral Raise
overview: Raise one cable handle out to the side at a time to isolate the side delts unilaterally.
coachingCues: Brace the torso before each rep.
Keep the hand slightly below the elbow through the raise.
Control the lowering phase back into the stretch.
commonMistakes: Leaning hard away from the cable without control.
Shrugging at the top.
Using torso rotation to finish reps.

name: Machine Lateral Raise
overview: Raise the machine arms out to the sides to isolate the side delts on a guided path.
coachingCues: Set the machine so the pads align with your elbows or forearms.
Drive the arms out without shrugging.
Control the return all the way down.
commonMistakes: Using momentum against the machine stops.
Shrugging through the top half.
Shortening the range to use more load.

name: Lean Away Cable Lateral Raise
overview: Lean away from the cable stack to increase the stretched range on a unilateral side-delt raise.
coachingCues: Use the support hand to create a stable lean.
Raise through the elbow while keeping the shoulder packed.
Stay controlled in the bottom stretch.
commonMistakes: Using the lean to swing the weight.
Shrugging the shoulder upward.
Letting the torso rotate during the raise.

name: Reverse Pec Deck
overview: Open the machine arms outward to isolate the rear delts with upper-back assistance.
coachingCues: Set the handles so the shoulders start slightly in front of the torso.
Drive the arms out wide without shrugging.
Pause briefly in the open position.
commonMistakes: Using the traps to yank the handles back.
Bending the elbows more as the set gets hard.
Letting the chest lift off the pad.

name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
overview: Sweep the cable handles outward to isolate the rear delts with continuous tension.
coachingCues: Keep a slight elbow bend and hold it constant.
Open the arms wide by moving through the shoulders.
Keep the neck relaxed and shoulders down.
commonMistakes: Turning it into a row.
Shrugging up during the fly.
Using too much body sway to move the load.

name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
overview: Raise dumbbells out wide from a hinged position to isolate the rear delts.
coachingCues: Hinge to a stable torso angle first.
Move the elbows wide instead of rowing them back.
Keep the shoulders away from the ears.
commonMistakes: Standing too upright and turning it into a shrug.
Swinging the torso to start reps.
Using more elbow flexion as fatigue rises.

name: Chest Supported Rear Delt Fly
overview: Perform rear-delt flyes against an incline bench to reduce momentum and isolate the rear delts.
coachingCues: Keep the chest fixed to the pad.
Lift out wide in a smooth arc.
Control the lowering phase fully.
commonMistakes: Lifting the chest off the bench.
Turning the rep into a row.
Shrugging at the top.

name: Rear Delt Row
overview: Row with a wide elbow path to bias the rear delts and upper back.
coachingCues: Hinge and keep the torso fixed.
Pull the elbows out in line with the shoulders.
Keep the shoulder blades moving but do not shrug hard.
commonMistakes: Pulling too low and turning it into a lat row.
Using body English to move the weight.
Shrugging into the neck at the top.

name: Arnold Press
overview: Press dumbbells overhead with a rotating path to train the front delts and triceps.
coachingCues: Rotate smoothly rather than rushing the turn.
Keep the elbows under control through the bottom.
Press overhead without overextending the lower back.
commonMistakes: Using too much load and losing the rotation path.
Letting the elbows flare too early.
Arching hard to finish the press.

name: Single Arm Machine Shoulder Press
